behaviors.description: Process files created in subdirectories under the watched root, not just direct children.
behaviors.justification: Recursive watch behavior is an OS/filesystem event boundary. In-process mocks can miss real watcher semantics (missed nested events, path normalization differences, backend limitations).
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against temp input/output dirs. Create a nested subdirectory under input and write a file there. Assert exactly one transformed output appears for that nested file path within timeout.

behaviors.description: Discard a second processing request for the same file path within the configured deduplication window.
behaviors.justification: The likely failure is at module wiring (watch event handler + dedupe cache + scheduler), not raw OS behavior. A pure unit test of only the cache can miss path canonicalization and timing integration bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real dedupe/scheduler modules with a fake clock and stub transformer. Submit two requests for the same canonical path within the dedupe window; verify one transform call. Advance clock past window and submit again; verify second call occurs.

behaviors.description: When OS emits multiple filesystem events for one write, process that file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly about real filesystem event fan-out. Mocked watcher events can hide backend-specific duplicate patterns and timing races.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watcher on temp dirs. Perform a write pattern known to emit multiple events (create + append + close/rename) for one file. Assert only one processing invocation/output for that path.

behaviors.description: If watched directory is deleted and recreated while running, daemon reattaches and continues without restart.
behaviors.justification: Directory deletion/recreation and watch re-registration are OS watcher lifecycle behaviors. Mocks typically do not reproduce dropped handles/inode replacement issues.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real temp directory. Delete the watched directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file. Assert daemon processes the new file without process restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive simultaneously, process in creation-timestamp order.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is ordering logic across intake queue + metadata lookup modules. Full OS-level timing can add noise; deterministic integration setup can validate real ordering policy implementation.
behaviors.test_description: Use real queue/sorter modules with controlled file metadata inputs (or deterministic fs fixture) for multiple arrivals in same batch. Assert processing sequence matches ascending creation timestamp, including tie-break rule if defined.

behaviors.description: A file still being written when arrival event fires is processed only after writing completes.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real write timing and filesystem visibility (partial writes, size stabilization, close semantics). Mocks often hide these race conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on real temp dirs. Open a file and write chunks with delays while keeping it in-progress. Verify daemon does not process partial content; after final write/close, verify single processing run with full final content.
